user.title: Choose Entities
user.description:
Select two or more entities to track distances between. Supported types: person, device tracker, sensor, or zone.

For a group of 3 or more, all pairwise distances are tracked automatically. After setup, sensors will show 'Unknown' until entities have reported a location.

data_description.entities: Select 2–5 entities to track. Each pair will get its own set of distance and proximity sensors.

distances.title: Distance Settings
distances.description: Set the zone boundaries and choose which zone triggers the proximity alert.

data_description.zone_very_near_m: Entities are 'Very Near' at or closer than this distance. Default: 200 m.
data_description.zone_near_m: Entities are 'Near' at or closer than this distance. Must be greater than Very Near. Default: 1000 m.
data_description.zone_mid_m: Entities are at 'Medium' distance at or closer than this. Must be greater than Near. Default: 5000 m.
data_description.zone_far_m:
Entities are 'Far' at or closer than this distance. Must be greater than Medium. Default: 20000 m.
Beyond this is 'Very Far'.
data_description.proximity_zone: The 'In Proximity' sensor turns ON when entities are within this zone or closer (e.g. selecting 'Near' also triggers when entities are 'Very Near'). It turns OFF when they move to the next zone out.
data_description.show_advanced: Turn on to configure GPS accuracy, speed, and reliability filters on the next screen. Leave off to use sensible defaults.

advanced.title: Advanced Filters
advanced.description: These filters help ignore bad GPS data. The defaults work well for most users — only change these if you're seeing false triggers.

data.require_reliable: Only trigger when data is reliable

data_description.max_accuracy_m: Maximum GPS uncertainty radius. Updates with worse accuracy are ignored. Set to 0 to accept all updates.
data_description.debounce_s: Wait this many seconds after a location update before recalculating. Reduces rapid toggling on bouncy GPS. 0 = react instantly.
data_description.grace_window_s: How long to show last-known sensor values after GPS signal is lost before reporting unknown. Range: 60–3600 s.
data_description.max_speed_kmh: Reject location updates that imply movement faster than this speed. Filters GPS teleport jumps. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.require_reliable: When on, the 'In Proximity' sensor only turns ON after the location has been consistent across several consecutive updates. Reduces false positives caused by a single bad GPS fix.
data_description.min_updates_reliable: How many consecutive updates are needed before the location is considered reliable. Only applies when 'Only trigger when data is reliable' is on.
data_description.resync_silence_s: If all tracked entities stop sending GPS updates for this long, proximity state is frozen to prevent false changes. Range: 60–3600 s.
data_description.resync_hold_s: How long to hold proximity state frozen after GPS silence is detected. Range: 0–300 s.

error.duplicate_entities: Each entity must appear only once.
error.zone_thresholds_not_ascending: Zone thresholds must increase in order: Very Near < Near < Medium < Far.

abort.already_configured: This entity pair is already configured.
abort.already_in_progress: A setup is already in progress for this integration. Close and reopen the dialog to start again.

distances.title: Distance Settings
distances.description: Set the zone boundaries and choose which zone triggers the proximity alert.

data_description.zone_very_near_m: Entities are 'Very Near' at or closer than this distance. Default: 200 m.
data_description.zone_near_m: Entities are 'Near' at or closer than this distance. Must be greater than Very Near. Default: 1000 m.
data_description.zone_mid_m: Entities are at 'Medium' distance at or closer than this. Must be greater than Near. Default: 5000 m.
data_description.zone_far_m:
Entities are 'Far' at or closer than this distance. Must be greater than Medium. Default: 20000 m.
Beyond this is 'Very Far'.
data_description.proximity_zone: The 'In Proximity' sensor turns ON when entities are within this zone or closer (e.g. selecting 'Near' also triggers when entities are 'Very Near'). It turns OFF when they move to the next zone out.
data_description.show_advanced: Turn on to configure GPS accuracy, speed, and reliability filters on the next screen. Leave off to use sensible defaults.

advanced.title: Advanced Filters
advanced.description: These filters help ignore bad GPS data. The defaults work well for most users — only change these if you're seeing false triggers.

data.require_reliable: Only trigger when data is reliable

data_description.debounce_s: After a location update arrives, how long to wait before recalculating. Useful when phones send several noisy GPS fixes in a row — the integration waits for them to settle. 0 = react instantly (default). Raise to 5–15 s if you see jittery on/off switching.
data_description.max_accuracy_m: Ignore location updates where the GPS error is larger than this. Phones in motion typically report 100–250 m accuracy, so 300 m is a safe default. A phone showing '350 m accuracy' would be skipped. Set to 0 to turn this off.
data_description.max_speed_kmh: Teleport guard. Rejects a GPS fix if the implied movement speed — after subtracting the combined GPS accuracy of both devices — exceeds this value. With 150 m accuracy on each phone, a jump must imply movement beyond this speed above the noise to be filtered. Default: 1000 km/h — blocks impossible teleports while allowing planes. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.require_reliable: When on, the 'In Proximity' sensor only turns ON after the location has been consistent across several consecutive updates. Reduces false positives caused by a single bad GPS fix. Note: if updates are infrequent (e.g. every 3 min), this can add several minutes of delay.
data_description.min_updates_reliable: How many consecutive updates are needed before the location is considered reliable. Only applies when 'Only trigger when data is reliable' is on. Example: 3 updates at 3-minute intervals = up to 9 minutes before the sensor turns ON.

error.zone_thresholds_not_ascending: Zone thresholds must increase in order: Very Near < Near < Medium < Far.
